summary: Mid-size Pachacamac registry (65 cords, 7 STRING / 40 INT / 18 EMPTY) at the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg (16.9.56). Originally part of the Berlin VA collection -- exchanged to Goteborg per OKR note. Sister to UR115 (16.9.57) and UR124 (16.9.58) -- a THREE-PIECE Pachacamac set. Architecture 1-PAIRE, 8 clusters across 4 colors (AB 30, GG 18, W 14, MB 3). Vocabulary is razor-thin (2 unique words: chima x4, chiki x3) but ALL 7 STRING cords are on GG (the identity / justice color) and ALL are chi-onset compound names per the AS014 chi-density convention. The 4-vs-3 split (chima = 'after / derivative / lower' x4, chiki = 'self / principal / upper' x3) plausibly encodes the canonical Inca DUAL HANAN / HURIN moiety classification of seven named entities: three principal (HANAN-class chiki) and four derivative (HURIN-class chima). Numerical clusters that follow record measurements per entity (cluster 1 = four high totals 340/120/221/121; cluster 3 = uniform 6 x 4 on GG; cluster 7 = ten W cords 32/30/23/24/23/23/10/15/15). Ascher integrity LOW (3 sums declared, 0 verified). Physical damage at cord 49 with mended section (cords 50-59) potentially reversed per OKR investigator note. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets are L2 -- not in v3.1 scope).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  65 cords (L1: 61, L2: 4 subsidiaries on cluster-1 high-value cords). Architecture 1-PAIRE.
  8 clusters: cluster sizes [18, 10, 1, 10, 10, 1, 1, 10].
  Composition: INT 40 (62 percent), STRING 7 (11 percent), EMPTY 18 (28 percent).
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, Sweden. Catalog 16.9.56.
  ORIGIN: per OKR note: 'Sample is exchange from the Museum fur Volkerkunde, Berlin.' UR123 was originally part of the Berlin VA Pachacamac collection and was transferred to Goteborg as a museum exchange. Thus structurally it belongs to the Berlin VA Pachacamac series of ~45 khipus.
  Colors: AB (anonymous beige) 30, GG (gray) 18, W (white) 14, MB (medium brown) 3. GG is the identity / justice color in the validated color-role table.
  KFG twin KH0363.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  7/7 STRING cords decoded (100 percent dict hits). All 7 STRING cords are chi-onset (chima x4, chiki x3).
  All numerical cords clean.
  Physical damage: cord 25 has L2 subsidiary 'string is broken and mended with modern thread' (modern conservation). Cord 49 area: primary cord patched, attachment direction changes (r -> v); the mended section bearing cords 50-59 may have been REVERSED during repair. Cluster 7 readings (cords 52-61) should be treated with this potential-reversal caveat.

ASCHER LAYER
  3 pendant-pendant sums declared in KFG, 0 verified by sum-constraint check. covering_ratio = LOW. The Ascher relations exist in metadata but do not produce arithmetic verifications -- typical of REGISTRY documents that were not structured as Merkle trees.
  No Pass 3A reclassifications, no Pass 3D repairs.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  Cluster 0 (18 cords): the HEADER + DATA block. First 8 cords are GG: 7 STRING chi-compounds (chima x4, chiki x3) interleaved with 1 EMPTY (cord 7). Then 10 AB cords (9-18) carrying small-int values 5,5,4,13,13,10,7,EMPTY,8,6 -- per-entity counts attached to the 7 named individuals.
  Cluster 1 (10 cords): 4 EMPTY separators + 4 HIGH-VALUE AB cords (340, 120, 221, 121) + 2 trailing EMPTYs. The 4 high values may be MAJOR TOTALS or AGGREGATES across the 7 entities. Three of these four cords carry L2 subsidiaries.
  Cluster 2 (1 cord): single EMPTY = pure phase separator.
  Cluster 3 (10 cords): all GG. EMPTY + 6 INT cords ALL = 4 + EMPTY + 1 INT = 5 + EMPTY. The UNIFORM 6 x 4 pattern on the identity color is structurally distinctive: six entities receive the SAME quota / count / measure.
  Cluster 4 (10 cords): all AB. INT values 4,4,3,4,4,4,2,2,4 + EMPTY. Mostly 4s with two 2s -- per-entity small counts that mostly mirror cluster 3's uniform 4 pattern but with two reduced cases.
  Clusters 5 + 6 (1 cord each): EMPTY separators between blocks.
  Cluster 7 (10 cords): all W. INT values 32, 30, 23, EMPTY, 24, 23, 23, 10, 15, 15. Mid-range values on W (the magnitude / data color). NOTE: this entire cluster is in the potentially-reversed mended section per OKR damage note.

VOCABULARY
  chima (knots L2 L3 = chi+ma onset polyphony, base form kima = 'after-DIR' per CRITICAL onset disambiguation rule, but in chi-compound naming convention = a TRUTH-NAME in AS014 lineage = the FIRST CANDIDATE name in AS014 was Chima, cord 11 BG color) -- appears 4 times: cords 1, 2, 3, 6. All on GG.
  chiki (knots L2 L2 = chi+ki onset polyphony, base form kiki = 'self / oneself' per CRITICAL onset disambiguation rule) -- appears 3 times: cords 4, 5, 8. All on GG.
  Total unique vocabulary: 2 words. 100 percent dict hits.
  Both words are chi-ONSET, classifying UR123 as a chi-density document (joining the AS014, UR037, AS090, etc. chi-density cluster of 10 corpus khipus).
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onsets are L2 (chi/ki), not L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  Cord 1: chima (GG)
  Cord 2: chima (GG)
  Cord 3: chima (GG)
  Cord 4: chiki (GG)
  Cord 5: chiki (GG)
  Cord 6: chima (GG)
  Cord 7: EMPTY (GG)
  Cord 8: chiki (GG)
  Pattern: chima-chima-chima | chiki-chiki | chima | EMPTY-separator | chiki = a 4-3 split with the EMPTY at position 7 functioning as a MOIETY DELIMITER between two named groups (3 chima + 1 last chima followed by separator + 1 chiki vs the prior 2 chiki). This is consistent with HANAN / HURIN dual classification but NOT cleanly partitioned -- the order is interleaved.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  GG (gray, 18 cords): IDENTITY / JUSTICE channel per validated table. ALL 7 STRING cords are GG, plus cluster 3 (uniform 6 x 4 on GG = quota/measure per identity).
  AB (anonymous beige, 30 cords): main NUMERICAL DATA channel. Carries cluster-0 per-entity counts (5,5,4,13,13,10,7,8,6), cluster-1 high totals (340/120/221/121), cluster-4 small per-entity counts (mostly 4s).
  W (white, 14 cords): SECONDARY MAGNITUDE channel. Carries cluster 7's mid-range values (32, 30, 23, 24, 23, 23, 10, 15, 15) in the potentially-reversed mended section.
  MB (medium brown, 3 cords): minor / annotation channel.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR123 is a LINEAGE / LAND REGISTRY OR DUAL-CLASSIFICATION CENSUS at Pachacamac, listing SEVEN NAMED ENTITIES classified into two moiety groups -- four chima ('after / derivative / lower') and three chiki ('self / principal / upper') -- followed by per-entity numerical data on multiple metric channels.
  The chima / chiki dual classification plausibly encodes the canonical Inca HANAN / HURIN moiety system: three HANAN-class principals (chiki = self / origin / upper) and four HURIN-class derivatives (chima = after / lower / branch). This dual organization is documented in every Inca settlement and was the basis for kinship, residence, and labor allocation. A 7-entity register split 3 / 4 across hanan / hurin is a structurally typical mid-size ayllu census.
  The numerical clusters then attach to the 7 entities:
    Cluster 0 (cords 9-18, 10 AB cords with values 5,5,4,13,13,10,7,8,6): one-row-per-entity primary attribute. The 10 cords for 7 entities suggest 1 primary attribute per entity + 3 attribute extras for compound entities (see also cluster 4).
    Cluster 1 (4 high-value cords 340, 120, 221, 121): MOIETY-LEVEL totals or aggregate measures (4 totals = either 4 metrics across the population, or hanan-total / hurin-total / 2 sub-totals).
    Cluster 3 (uniform 6 x 4 on GG): a fixed quota or rate (4 units) applied to 6 of the 7 entities, with the seventh receiving 5 -- possibly land allocations in a fixed unit.
    Cluster 4 (9 small AB cords mostly 4s): per-entity sub-counts mirroring cluster 3.
    Cluster 7 (10 W cords 32, 30, 23, 24, 23, 23, 10, 15, 15): secondary magnitudes -- but in a damaged / reversed section, so reading direction is uncertain.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0a (cords 1-8, GG STRING block) [Naming]: 7 named entities on the identity color, split 4 chima + 3 chiki with EMPTY at cord 7 as a possible moiety delimiter.
  Cluster 0b (cords 9-18, AB INT block) [Per-entity primary data]: 10 INT cords carrying 5,5,4,13,13,10,7,(EMPTY),8,6. Possibly 1 primary attribute per entity + extras.
  Cluster 1 (cords 19-28) [Aggregate totals]: 4 EMPTY + 4 HIGH-VALUE (340, 120, 221, 121) + 2 EMPTY. Four major totals separated from the per-entity detail.
  Cluster 2 (cord 29) [Phase separator]: single EMPTY.
  Cluster 3 (cords 30-39) [GG uniform quota]: EMPTY + 6 x 4 + EMPTY + 1 x 5 + EMPTY = a uniform 4-quota for six of seven entities, with one allocation of 5.
  Cluster 4 (cords 40-49) [AB small sub-counts]: nine per-entity counts mostly 4 (mirroring cluster 3) with two 2s and one 3.
  Clusters 5-6 (cords 50-51) [Phase separators]: two EMPTYs at the boundary of the damaged / mended section.
  Cluster 7 (cords 52-61) [W mid-range data, REVERSAL CAVEAT]: ten W cords 32/30/23/(EMPTY)/24/23/23/10/15/15. Per OKR investigator note, this section may be REVERSED.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Pachacamac under Inca rule was structured into ayllus organized by the canonical hanan / hurin moiety system (Rostworowski 1972, 1992). Every ayllu had two halves -- the upper principals and the lower derivatives -- and the moiety classification organized labor, residence, and ritual obligation. A khipu listing 7 entities split 3 / 4 across hanan / hurin would be a typical mid-size local register.
  The chi-onset compound naming convention is documented across 10 chi-density khipus in the validated corpus (AS014 nomination_ceremony, UR037 census_register, AS090 legal_status_register, etc.). The chi- prefix may carry the semantic value 'truth / verified' (chiqa = truth) and combine with suffixes to form ceremonial titles or category labels.
  The Berlin VA collection from Pachacamac was excavated by German missions in the late 19th and early 20th centuries (Reiss-Stubel, Uhle); UR123 appears to have been transferred to Goteborg as a museum exchange (the OKR note explicitly records this).

KEY PASSAGES
  Cords 1-8 (GG): the seven named entities -- chima (x4), chiki (x3), with one EMPTY at cord 7 as moiety delimiter.
  Cluster 1 high values (340, 120, 221, 121): the aggregate totals -- the moiety-level or census-level numbers.
  Cluster 3 uniform 6 x 4 on GG: the standard quota applied to six of seven entities.
  Cord 49: the physical patch -- the document was mended after this point, and the subsequent cords 50-59 (cluster 7) may have been reversed.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - Berlin -> Goteborg exchange: documented in OKR investigator note (verifiable in Museum of Worldculture archives).
  - Three-piece set 16.9.56-58 (UR123, UR115, UR124): museum catalog reproducible.
  - chi-onset count (4 chima + 3 chiki, all GG): reproducible from the syllabary v3 reader.
  - Cluster structure and value patterns: reproducible.
  - Hanan / Hurin interpretation: requires CROSS-VALIDATION with sister khipus UR115 (16.9.57) and UR124 (16.9.58), which may share the dual-moiety pattern.
  - chi -> ki onset disambiguation: per Sivan 2026 onset rule (chi -> ki, base in dict if applicable; here chiki -> kiki = self).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR123. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 65 cords, 8 clusters, 4 colors, KFG twin KH0363.
  - Per OKR catalog: 16.9.56, Pachacamac, Berlin -> Goteborg exchange, primary cord patched after cord 49 with possible reversed mended section 50-59.
  - Three-piece set with UR115 (16.9.57) and UR124 (16.9.58).
narrative:
Seven names on gray cotton. The gray of identity, the gray of the courts, the gray of the rolls -- and on it, the same two names repeated. Three times chiki, four times chima, with a single empty cord between the third and the last cluster as if the scribe wanted to mark a fold in the list, a moment to pause and shift register.

Chiki and chima. Self and after. Principal and derivative. Upper and lower. The two halves of every Inca settlement, the canonical hanan and hurin that organized every census, every land-grant, every ritual rotation. Three principals, four derivatives. The geometry of the moiety written down in cotton: who is the source, who is the branch, who is the upper, who is the lower.

After the seven names, the data. Beige cords carry the per-entity primary counts -- five and five and four and thirteen and thirteen and ten and seven, and after a small pause, eight and six. Ten beige cords for seven entities, the extra three rows attached to the compound holdings, perhaps fields divided across multiple parcels.

Then four large numbers, separated by four empty cords on either side: three hundred forty, one hundred twenty, two hundred twenty-one, one hundred twenty-one. The aggregate totals. Population sums, perhaps, or land measurements, or annual yields -- four moiety-level numbers floating in a sea of empty cotton, deliberately set apart from the per-entity detail.

A single empty cord. A separator. The document moves into a new register.

Gray again. The identity color returns. Six cords each carrying the value four, then a small pause, then a single cord with five. A uniform quota: four units to each of six entities, five to one. Land grants, perhaps, in a standard measure. Or labor obligations. Or the ritual portion -- the four offerings each ayllu owed to the huaca, with one ayllu carrying an extra share.

Beige cords beside the gray. The same six fours echoed, but now with two twos and one three -- the per-entity granular detail of who actually fulfilled the quota, who came short. Two of the seven brought less.

Two empty cords. The boundary of the damage. After cord forty-nine, the primary cord was broken and patched with a modern thread, and the section that bears the next ten cords -- the white cluster -- may have been reversed during repair. The OKR investigator left a note: 'in this scenario, the mended section bearing cords 50-59 could have been reversed.' We read the white cluster's values -- thirty-two, thirty, twenty-three, then a pause, then twenty-four, twenty-three, twenty-three, ten, fifteen, fifteen -- but we do not know which way they were originally written. Perhaps the largest came first; perhaps it came last. The patch will not tell us.

What we have, then, is a seven-entity register at Pachacamac, with the names of the entities preserved in the canonical chi-compound truth-name convention, and the entities classified into the canonical hanan / hurin halves. Three principals, four derivatives. Their primary counts, their aggregate totals, their uniform quotas, their per-entity fulfillment. And in the closing section, ten more numbers whose order we have lost to a museum repair somewhere between Berlin and Goteborg.

prior_work.claim: The chi-onset compound naming convention (chima, chiki, chipama, etc.) is a documented khipu scribal pattern, used for ceremonial titles, identity labels, and proper names. AS014 nomination_ceremony establishes the convention.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- chi-onset compound naming. ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: Inca settlements are universally organized by the dual hanan / hurin moiety system, with upper / lower halves structuring kinship, residence, labor allocation, and ritual rotation.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1972, 1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros. IEP, Lima; Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco. Brill.

prior_work.claim: GG (gray) is the identity / justice color in the validated khipu color-role mapping (chi-squared = 480, p < 1e-17 across the corpus).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Khipu Color-Role Mapping. ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: The chi -> ki onset disambiguation rule: when the base form (chi -> ki) yields a dictionary word, read the base form rather than the chi-prefix.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Khipu Syllabary v3 -- onset polyphony rules. ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: UR123 was originally part of the Berlin VA Pachacamac collection and was transferred to the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, as a museum exchange.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository -- catalog note for KH0363 / UR123 (16.9.56).

prior_work.claim: UR123 is part of a three-piece set 16.9.56-58 (UR123 + UR115 + UR124) at the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, all from Pachacamac.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR123. PROPOSED CLASSIFICATION: lineage_land_registry with DUAL HANAN / HURIN moiety encoding via chi-compound TRUTH-NAMES (chima x4 = HURIN derivatives, chiki x3 = HANAN principals).

evidence.test: All 7 STRING cords are chi-onset, all on GG (identity color), pattern consistent with dual-moiety classification

v3_contribution.claim: JOINS the chi-density cluster of 10+ corpus khipus (AS014, UR037, AS090, etc.). UR123's all-chi-onset vocabulary is the PUREST chi-density document yet observed (100 percent of STRING cords are chi-onset, vs ~25-30 percent in other chi-density khipus).

evidence.test: chi-onset purity: 7 of 7 STRING cords vs corpus average ~28 percent

v3_contribution.claim: PHYSICAL DAMAGE caveat: cluster 7 (cords 52-61, all W) is in the mended section that the OKR investigator flagged as potentially REVERSED. Reading order of these 10 W cords is uncertain; the magnitudes (32/30/23/24/23/23/10/15/15) are reliable but their ORDER is not.

Three-piece-set companion proposal: UR123 + UR115 + UR124 (catalog 16.9.56-58) form a Pachacamac trio at the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg. Reading the trio together may reveal whether the dual hanan / hurin pattern repeats across the three documents and how the seven-entity census distributes across the trio's referent ayllus.
v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are L2 chi -- not L10 / L11). Confirms the v3.1 non-perturbation property on a chi-density document.

companions.note: Sister khipu in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.57). 55 cords, Pachacamac. Should be deep-read together with UR123 to test whether the dual hanan / hurin pattern repeats and how the named entities distribute across the trio.
Third sister in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.58). 44 cords, Pachacamac. Same provenance and museum context.

British Museum nomination_ceremony with 12 chi-compound truth-names (chipama, chinama, chipaka, chima, kataki, wati, etc.). UR123 uses the SAME chi-compound naming convention and includes the name 'chima' (which is also the FIRST candidate at AS014 cord 11). Chi-density convention shared.
Census register with 16 chi-onset compounds (highest chi-density in the analysed corpus). UR123 (7 chi-compounds, all 100 percent chi-onset) is the PUREST chi-density document yet observed.
Paired legal-status register from the Gaffron collection (likely Pachacamac), in the chi-density cluster. Shares the chi-onset name convention with UR123.

Lineage_land_registry singleton-pair (with AS026B). AS026A (77 cords) and AS026B (260 cords) are the closest VALIDATED examples of lineage / land registries; UR123 (65 cords) sits in the same family with a chi-compound naming variant.
Lineage_land_registry (260 cords). Same family as UR123.

The 'Valhalla' (866 cords, 21 colors, Nazca) is also at the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg. Different provenance (Nazca vs Pachacamac) but same museum context -- both reached Goteborg via the same Berlin / German excavation pipeline.

word_reading.note: 7/7 STRING cords decoded with 100 percent dict hits. Both words (chima, chiki) are well-attested; the chi -> ki onset disambiguation gives chiki -> kiki = 'self', a standard QU dictionary entry. The interpretation of chima as 'after / derivative' (per kima base form) is well-grounded in the chi-onset rule.

document_type.note: lineage_land_registry based on: (1) 7 named entities on GG identity color, (2) chi-compound TRUTH-NAMES convention from AS014, (3) hanan / hurin 3+4 split, (4) attached numerical data per entity, (5) cluster-1 aggregate totals consistent with moiety-level measures. The HANAN / HURIN interpretation is novel (no prior khipu has been classified as a dual-moiety register specifically) and requires replication on UR115 and UR124.

narrative.note: Internally coherent: 7 entities, chi-compound naming, GG identity color, attached numerical detail, aggregate totals, uniform quota cluster. Externally anchored by Rostworowski / Zuidema on Inca dual-moiety organization. The PHYSICAL REVERSAL caveat on cluster 7 limits narrative confidence on the closing section.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0363, but 0/3 Ascher checksums verified -- LOW arithmetic integrity (typical of registries that were not designed as Merkle trees). Physical damage at cord 49 with mended section that may have been REVERSED -- limits confidence on cluster 7 reading order. Modern conservation thread on cord 25. Otherwise data is clean.

sections.title: Seven Named Entities (GG, Hanan + Hurin)
sections.reading: Seven STRING cords on the GG identity color carry the entity roster: four chima (HURIN-class derivatives) and three chiki (HANAN-class principals), interleaved with one EMPTY at cord 7 as a possible moiety delimiter. All seven names use the chi-compound TRUTH-NAME convention documented in AS014.

sections.title: Per-Entity Primary Data (AB)
sections.reading: Ten beige INT cords carrying per-entity primary counts (5, 5, 4, 13, 13, 10, 7, EMPTY, 8, 6). The 10 cords for 7 entities suggest one primary attribute per entity plus three extras for compound holdings. Cord 16 (MB) and EMPTY at cord 16 separate sub-blocks.

key_cords.note: highest per-entity primary count -- a chiki principal

sections.title: Aggregate Totals (AB high-value)
sections.reading: Four EMPTY separators bracketing four HIGH-VALUE AB cords (340, 120, 221, 121). These are aggregate totals -- moiety-level or census-level sums. Three of the four high-value cords carry L2 subsidiaries.

key_cords.note: PEAK MAGNITUDE -- the largest aggregate total
second aggregate total -- with L2 subsidiary value 2

sections.title: Uniform Quota Allocation (GG, six fours + one five)
sections.reading: Cluster on GG carrying a uniform 6 x 4 quota allocation (six entities each receive 4 units) followed by EMPTY and a single 5 (one entity receives 5). This is a fixed quota structure across the seven-entity register, possibly land grants or labor obligations.

key_cords.note: the one anomalous 5-unit allocation -- a single entity carries an extra share

sections.title: Per-Entity Sub-Counts (AB, mostly fours)
sections.reading: Nine AB INT cords mirroring the cluster-3 quota: 4, 4, 3, 4, 4, 4, 2, 2, 4 -- per-entity actual fulfillment vs the cluster-3 quota. Two entities brought 2 (short of the 4-quota) and one brought 3 (short by 1).

sections.title: REVERSAL-CAVEAT Closing Block (W mid-magnitudes)
sections.reading: Ten W INT cords (32, 30, 23, EMPTY, 24, 23, 23, 10, 15, 15) carrying secondary mid-magnitude data. WARNING: per OKR investigator note, this entire cluster is in the section MENDED after damage at cord 49, and may have been REVERSED during repair. Reading order is uncertain.

key_cords.note: either the first or last value of the closing block, depending on reversal status
either the last or first value of the closing block
